codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the array parsing bug identified in the prior review of wave4-memory-outcomes-yield-cooldown. A new parseJsonArray<T> helper was added that properly handles JSON array payloads (returning [] for invalid input instead of {}), and mapExploitOutcomeRow now uses it for rejectionSamples instead of parseJsonObject. The VAL-LOOP-MEM-002 test was updated with explicit assertions verifying rejectionSamples round-trip across store reopen. The fix is minimal, well-scoped, and all 8 targeted tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: discovery-memory-store.ts now has three JSON parsing helpers with distinct purposes: parseJsonObject (returns {} for non-objects), parseJsonArray (returns [] for non-arrays), and parseStringArray (returns [] with normalization). Future workers adding new fields to SQLite rows should consult these helpers before using safeParseJson directly. This pattern is not yet documented in the library.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: discovery-memory-store.ts:2638-2645 defines parseJsonArray<T>, discovery-memory-store.ts:2630-2637 defines parseJsonObject, and parseStringArray is also defined nearby. All three use safeParseJson as the underlying parser.

summary: PASS — The fix is clean and directly addresses the array-round-trip data corruption issue. parseJsonArray<T> correctly handles JSON array payloads (returning [] instead of {} for non-array values), and mapExploitOutcomeRow now uses it for rejectionSamples. The VAL-LOOP-MEM-002 test has explicit round-trip assertions for both rejectionSamples arrays after store reopen. The fix is minimal (16 insertions, 9 deletions across 2 files), well-scoped, and all 8 targeted tests + typecheck + lint pass. No issues found.
